title: 16s Metagenomic Sequencing using Illumina iSeq100 Platform
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"em5h4","text":"The characterization of microbial communities through 16S rRNA gene sequencing is a cornerstone of microbiome research. While established protocols are available for larger Illumina platforms, there remains a lack of published workflows optimized for the compact and cost-effective Illumina iSeq100 system. Here, we present a standardized protocol for 16S metagenomic sequencing using the iSeq100, detailing sample preparation, library construction, and sequencing parameters. Amplicons targeting the V4 hypervariable regions of the 16S rRNA gene with a size of ~300 bp were generated using a dual-indexed PCR approach adapted for the iSeq100’s 2 × 150 bp read chemistry. Library quality was evaluated through fluorometric quantification and fragment analysis prior to sequencing. Using this workflow, the iSeq100 produced high-quality reads suitable for accurate taxonomic profiling, with sufficient depth for community structure assessment across diverse environmental and biological samples. Results demonstrate that the iSeq100 is a reliable and accessible platform for small to medium-scale 16S metagenomic studies, providing consistent performance while reducing sequencing costs and turnaround time. This protocol fills a current gap in published methodologies, enabling broader adoption of the iSeq100 for microbiome research and routine microbial community monitoring.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Vortex the AMPure XP beads for 30 seconds to make sure that the beads are evenly dispersed. Add an appropriate volume of beads to a reservoir depending on the number of samples being processed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Label a new PCR plate with PCR1, DATE, and APPLICATION (Ex: PCR1 04/26/2015 16S Metagenomics).
Prepare a mastermix using the Q5 NEB High Fidelity PCR Master mix as follows: %s %s
Load 24 μl PCR mastermix into each well of the PCR1 Plate.
Add 1 μl gDNA into the mastermix.

Seal the plate, place on a thermal cycler and run the PCR1 Program: 98°C for 30 seconds 30 cycles of: 98°C for 10 seconds 55°C for 30 seconds 72°C for 30 seconds 72°C for 2 minutes Hold at 4°C Set the reaction volume to 25 μL

Add 20 μl of AMPure XP beads to each well of the PCR1 plate. Change tips in between samples. Gently pipette the entire volume up and down 10 times. Incubate at room temperature without shaking for 5 minutes. Place the plate on a magnetic stand for 2 minutes or until the supernatant has cleared. Use a pipette to remove and discard the supernatant. Change tips between samples.

With the PCR1 plate on the magnetic stand, wash the beads with freshly prepared 80% ethanol as follows: Using a pipette, add 100 μl of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each sample well. Incubate the plate on the magnetic stand for 30 seconds. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.

With the PCR1 plate on the magnetic stand, perform a second ethanol wash as follows: Using a pipette, add 100 μl of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each sample well. Incubate the plate on the magnetic stand for 30 seconds. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Using a filtered tip, remove excess ethanol.
With the PCR1 plate still on the magnetic stand, allow the beads to air dry for 10 minutes. Remove the PCR 1 plate from the magnetic stand.
Add 25 μl of 10 mM Tris pH 8.5 to each well of the PCR1 plate.
Gently pipette mix up and down 10 times, changing tips after each column (or seal plate and shake at 1800 rpm for 2 minutes). Make sure that beads are fully resuspended.
Incubate at room temperature for 2 minutes.
Place the plate on the magnetic stand for 2 minutes or until the supernatant has cleared.
Label new PCR plate PCR CLEAN-UP 1, DATE and APPLICATION
Carefully transfer 20 μl of the supernatant from the PCR 1 plate to the PCR CLEAN-UP 1 plate. Change tips between samples to avoid cross contamination.
Perform gel electrophoresis to view the PCR Amplicons from the PCR CLEAN-UP 1 plate.
SAFE STOPPING POINT. Seal plate and store at -25°C for up to 7 days.
Label a new PCR plate INDEX PCR and DATE
Prepare a mastermix as follows: %s %s
Transfer 10 µl master mix on each well of the new INDEX PCR plate. Gently pipette up and down 10 times to mix.
Add 10 μl index adapters to each well.
. Add 5 μl PCR product from the PCR CLEAN-UP 1 Plate to each well. Cover the plate with plastic sealing film. Centrifuge the plate at 1,000 × g at 20°C for 1 minute.

Place on a thermal cycler and run the INDEX PCR Program using the following conditions: 95°C for 3 minutes 8 cycles of: 95°C for 30 seconds 56°C for 30 seconds 72°C for 30 seconds 72°C for 5 minutes Hold at 4°C
CONSUMABLES AND REAGENTS %s %s
%s %s

THINGS TO DO BEFORE DOING INDEX PCR CLEAN UP: Centrifuge the INDEX PCR plate at 280 × g at room temperature for 1 minute to collect condensation. Vortex the AMPure XP beads for 30 seconds to make sure that the beads are evenly dispersed. Add an appropriate volume of beads to a reservoir.

3. Add 56 μl of AMPure XP beads to each well of the INDEX PCR plate. Gently pipette mix up and down 10 times. Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes. Place the plate on a magnetic stand for 2 minutes or until the supernatant has cleared.
With the INDEX PCR plate on the magnetic stand, use a pipette to remove and discard the supernatant. Change tips between samples.

With the INDEX PCR plate on the magnetic stand, wash the beads with freshly prepared 80% ethanol as follows: Add 100 μl of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each sample well. Incubate the plate on the magnetic stand for 30 seconds. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.

With the INDEX PCR plate on the magnetic stand, perform a second ethanol wash as follows: Add 100 μl of freshly prepared 80% ethanol to each sample well. Incubate the plate on the magnetic stand for 30 seconds. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant. Use a pipette with fine pipette tips to remove excess ethanol. Allow the beads to air dry for 10 minutes. Remove the Index PCR plate from the magnetic stand.
Add 27.5 μl of 10 mM Tris pH 8.5 to each well of the INDEX PCR plate. Gently mix up and down 10 times until beads are fully resuspended, changing tips after each column.
Incubate at room temperature for 2 minutes. Place the plate on the magnetic stand for 2 minutes or until the supernatant has cleared. Label a new PCR plate PCR CLEAN-UP 2, and DATE
Carefully transfer 25 μl of the supernatant from the INDEX PCR plate to the new PCR CLEAN-UP 2 plate. Change tips between samples to avoid cross contamination.
Perform gel electrophoresis to view the PCR Amplicons from the PCR CLEAN-UP 2 plate.
SAFE STOPPING POINT. SEAL PLATE AND STORE AT -25°C for up to 7 days
Pool libraries as follows: Transfer 5 μl of cleaned up PCR amplicons from each well of the PCR CLEAN-UP 2 plate into a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube ( Label as Pooled Lib/PL ).
Analyze 1 µl library pool , NTC and PC using a Qubit dsDNA HS Assay kit. If libraries are outside the standard range, dilute to 1:10 concentration, and analyze again.
Analyze 1 µl library pool , NTC and PC using an Agilent Bioanalyzer DNA 1000 Assay Kit.
Calculate the molarity value using the following formula:
Dilute library to 4nM, then to final library concentration of 100 pM .
Tear open the foil packaging of the cartridge.
Tilt the cartridge 5 times.
Using a pipette tip, pierce the foil of the library reservoir.
Load 20 µl of diluted library with final concentration of 100 pM at the bottom of the library reservoir.
Insert the flow cell into the cartridge.
Load the cartridge with flow cells into the sequencer.
Wait for 19-24 hours until run is finished and analyze.
